hero.contrastLead: From playing live improv music for contemporary dance to earning a minor in Family Life, I'm not your typical guy in tech.
hero.professionalSentence: I bring that same practical attention to detail, empathy, and collaboration to everything I build.

handshakeLine: Working on something meaningful? I'd love to connect.

timeline.label: Earned Eagle Scout — the project required planning, leadership, follow-through, sewing skills.
Busser at The Old Spaghetti Factory — 600 guests a night, ten-person team, no slack. Learned what it feels like when coordination fails and everyone notices immediately.

Full-time Volunteer Representative for The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints in Albuquerque, NM for two years. A peer later said he never heard me speak a scripted word. I was serious about being authentic, thinking for myself, and speaking the people's language.
Joined Brigham Young University Department of Dance as a Contemporary Dance Accompanist, improvising live music for 15–75 dancers across 4 years using 13 instruments and music software.

I started my Bachelor of Science in Information Systems program at Brigham Young University. I deliberately chose an unfamiliar path to challenge myself. I took many opporutities to expand my perspective, such as studying abroad in the United Kingdom, Switzerland, and Italy, taking diverse elective courses, and earning a minor in Family Life.
Web Developer at Brigham Young University — built and maintained pages for campus PR and inclusivity initiatives.
First Teaching Assistant role at BYU Marriott School of Business — facilitated labs and reviews for 60 students covering Python, PostgreSQL, Django, HTML/CSS, and OOP.

INTEX 5-day project at BYU: consolidated and normalized 7 data sources, built a working web app in C#, ASP.NET, and AWS, and presented a fully functional archeological fieldwork data entry system — in a week.

WordPress Developer at BYU College of Fine Arts and Communications — audited 50+ undocumented workflow files, cut storage 19% across 8 department sites, and trained my replacement. First time I left something cleaner than I found it.

Consultant Intern at The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints — researched data sources for a world crisis information management tool; prototyped a solution that surfaced the organization's structural advantages.
Graduated with a B.S. in Information Systems from Brigham Young University. Received the Alumni Choice Award for integrity. Studied abroad across the United Kingdom, Switzerland, and Italy.
Full Stack Development Intern at OCSC — shipped 20+ full-stack changes to a production SaaS web application in six months.

UX and Web Developer at The LTN Foundation — led development of a responsive React site, designed and normalized a relational database, and clarified the organization's branding and information architecture.
Partnered with a local business to design and test agentic AI systems using LangChain — first time taking a real client's workflow friction and resolving it end-to-end with AI tooling.
Engineered a Python extraction pipeline processing 6.7M+ words of YouTube transcript data into AI-ready structured context — first large-scale data project built entirely solo.
Delivered AI B2B growth recommendations at the BYU Marriott Case Competition — earned Best of Room.
Data & Systems Teaching Assistant at BYU Marriott — taught 50+ graduate students Excel statistics, Tableau, data lifecycle, and SQL; debugged student work across five languages in TA labs.

Master of Information Systems Management, BYU Marriott School of Business. Emphasis in Data Engineering and Business Intelligence. Coursework: Agentic AI Systems, Data Engineering (Python, dbt, Snowflake), Data Science (R, scikit-learn), Cloud Engineering (AWS, Terraform).

projects.name: Soundwatch
projects.objective: Morning routines with multiple timed activities meant constantly starting timers instead of staying present.
projects.tradeOff: I chose elapsed time over countdown because focus should stay on the activity, not how much is left; that meant the UX had to work harder than a standard countdown timer to feel natural.
projects.previewAlt: Soundwatch interval timer running in the browser

projects.name: I AM
projects.objective: People struggle to articulate identity and goals in a way AI coaching products can act on consistently.
projects.tradeOff: We chose AWS Bedrock over OpenAI because the client needed enterprise-grade auditability and the knowledge base tooling fit their existing infrastructure, accepting a steeper learning curve and thinner community documentation.
projects.previewAlt: I AM product demo video thumbnail on YouTube

projects.name: YouTube Transcript Pipeline
projects.objective: Hours of spoken content in YouTube playlists are locked in the UI: not queryable, exportable, or usable for analysis.
projects.tradeOff: I chose to build a custom local pipeline to get fine-grained control over chunking and metadata for AI ingestion. It cost me more time than using a service, but provided me experience, complete control, and flexibility in end-goal use cases.
projects.previewAlt: YouTube Transcript Pipeline CLI and DuckDB workflow
